assistant_summary: {"findings":[{"title":"[P2] Persist the replacement decision when superseding","body":"`new_decision_id` is mandatory here and you resolve it successfully, but then the value is discarded. After `decision supersede <old> <new>`, the only persisted change is `old.staleness_status='superseded'`, so any later lookup loses which decision actually replaced it and the command is effectively indistinguishable from a plain status update.","confidence_score":0.98,"priority":2,"code_location":{"absolute_file_path":"/Users/teslamint/workspace/entirecontext/src/entirecontext/core/decisions.py","line_range":{"start":476,"end":487}}},{"title":"[P2] Mark stale decisions during `stale-all`","body":"This bulk command only prints decisions that look stale; it never writes `staleness_status='stale'` back to the database. In workflows that rely on `list_decisions(..., staleness_status='stale')` or `ec decision list --staleness stale`, running `stale-all` has no persistent effect, so the same decisions remain in the `fresh` set on the next command.","confidence_score":0.95,"priority":2,"code_location":{"absolute_file_path":"/Users/teslamint/workspace/entirecontext/src/entirecontext/cli/decisions_cmds.py","line_range":{"start":325,"end":333}}},{"title":"[P2] Base staleness on the decision timestamp","body":"Using a fixed `--since=1 month ago` window creates false positives whenever a linked file changed earlier in the same month but before the decision was recorded. For example, a decision created today for a file last touched last week will already be reported as stale even though nothing changed after the decision was made. The comparison needs to be relative to the decision's own creation/update time.","confidence_score":0.93,"priority":2,"code_location":{"absolute_file_path":"/Users/teslamint/workspace/entirecontext/src/entirecontext/core/decisions.py","line_range":{"start":551,"end":553}}}],"overall_correctness":"patch is incorrect","overall_explanation":"The patch introduces useful decision-management features, but the new supersede and stale workflows do not persist the right state, and staleness detection uses an unrelated calendar window that can mislabel new decisions. Those issues make the added commands return misleading results in normal usage.","overall_confidence_score":0.96}
